items.q: What is the difference between a strong acid and a concentrated acid?
items.correct: A strong acid fully dissociates in water; a concentrated acid has a high amount of acid per volume — these are independent properties.
items.misconception: Strong and concentrated mean the same thing for acids.
items.random_wrong: A strong acid is one that has been heated to higher temperature.
items.why: In everyday language 'strong' and 'concentrated' are synonyms (e.g. strong coffee), so students transfer that meaning to chemistry.

items.q: At room temperature and pressure, what volume does 1 mole of any gas occupy?
items.correct: Approximately 24 dm^3 (24,000 cm^3), regardless of the identity of the gas.
items.misconception: Heavier gases occupy a smaller volume per mole because their particles are denser.
items.random_wrong: Different gases occupy completely different volumes proportional to their molar mass.
items.why: Students assume heavier particles must be more compact, missing that gas volume depends only on number of particles, T and P (Avogadro's law).

items.q: What kind of bond forms between a ligand and a central metal ion in a complex?
items.correct: A dative (coordinate) covalent bond — the ligand donates a lone pair into an empty orbital on the metal.
items.misconception: The ligand forms an ionic bond with the metal because the metal is positively charged.
items.random_wrong: The ligand shares electrons equally with the metal as a normal covalent bond.
items.why: Students see the +ve metal and assume electrostatic ionic bonding, missing that ligands donate lone pairs into vacant d-orbitals.
It's mixture. “pure substance” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Why use pencil for the baseline?
items.why: It's graphite doesn't dissolve. “it is darker” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Solvent moves 15 cm, spot moves 6 cm. Rf?
items.why: Exact answer: 0.4. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
